title: EDR auth + authz
description: Authentication and authorization activity for the EDR server: audit decisions, denied actions, and auth rejections. Import via SigNoz: Dashboards -> New dashboard -> Import JSON. Requires the server to export OTLP with service.name=fleet-edr-server.

environment.name: environment
environment.description: Deployment environment to view, auto-discovered from the deployment.environment resource attribute. The fleet-edr server always emits deployment.environment (default value 'default', overridable via OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES) so this key exists in every SigNoz instance it reports to, which makes the dynamic lookup safe. Defaults to ALL so all environments show.

widgets.title: Audit events by decision
widgets.description: Every audited action grouped by its decision: allow (granted), deny (authz chokepoint refused), error (auth/OIDC failure), unspecified (context-free rows like enrollment.rotate_token or logout that carry no allow/deny). A baseline of allow plus occasional unspecified is healthy; any sustained deny or error series is the thing to chase.

filter.expression: resource.service.name = 'fleet-edr-server' AND edr.audit.action EXISTS AND deployment.environment IN $environment

widgets.title: Denied + errored audit events by action
widgets.description: Count of audit events whose decision is deny or error, grouped by action. This is the security signal: which privileged operations are being refused by the authz chokepoint (deny) or failing auth (error, e.g. auth.oidc.failure, auth.breakglass.failure). Cross-reference the HTTP dashboard's 401/403 series to attribute denies to specific routes.

filter.expression: resource.service.name = 'fleet-edr-server' AND edr.audit.decision IN ('deny', 'error') AND deployment.environment IN $environment

widgets.title: Auth rejections by reason
widgets.description: Bearer/session auth failures grouped by edr.auth.reason, set by the auth middleware on every 401 (server/httpserver/authfail.go). invalid_token / missing_bearer dominate when an agent is misconfigured or a token was revoked; a sudden spike from one source is the signal that caught the stray tokenless agent. Distinct from the authz deny panel above: this is authentication (who are you), that is authorization (may you).

filter.expression: resource.service.name = 'fleet-edr-server' AND edr.auth.reason EXISTS AND deployment.environment IN $environment

widgets.title: Audit activity by action
widgets.description: Total audited operations over the window, highest first. The operator audit trail at a glance: logins, alert triage, command issuance, enrollment revoke/rotate, application-control rule changes. Use it to spot anomalous volume (a burst of enrollment.revoke, an off-hours policy change) worth opening the trace for.

filter.expression: resource.service.name = 'fleet-edr-server' AND edr.audit.action EXISTS AND deployment.environment IN $environment
